system_prompt: You are an analyst. Read the brief carefully and provide your best-reasoned recommendation.
user_prompt:
You are presented with 5 anonymous responses (E1-E5) and 9 bipolar constructs. Rate each response on each construct on a 1-7 scale: 1 = strongly the LEFT pole, 7 = strongly the RIGHT pole, 4 = neutral or mixed.

Responses:
**E1:** I strongly recommend **Option D: Cloud processing with strict opt-in training**. For a direct-to-consumer health app with two million active users, the strategic mandate is to deliver a highly capable AI product without compromising consumer trust or destroying unit economics. Option D uniquely satisfies these competing demands.

**E2:** # Recommendation: Option B — Encrypted Cloud with No Training

For a health app serving 2M users, I recommend **Option B**: encrypted cloud processing in a confidential-computing enclave with a hard commitment that user data is never used for training. **Why this wins on the axes that matter most:**

Health data is among the most regulated and trust-sensitive categories of personal information. A direct-to-consumer health app's franchise depends not on having the cleverest model, but on users believing — correctly — that their data won't surface somewhere uncomfortable.

**E3:** I recommend **Option B: encrypted cloud processing with no training use** as the primary policy. For a direct-to-consumer health app, the core strategic asset is not just AI capability; it is user trust. Health data is among the most sensitive consumer data categories, and the AI coach will likely handle symptoms, habits, medications, reproductive health, mental health, and other high-risk information.

**E4:** For a health app with 2M users launching AI coaching, trust isn’t just a feature—it’s existential. I recommend **Option D: Cloud with opt-in training only**, because it delivers full AI capability while anchoring trust through transparent, affirmative user consent. Health data demands the highest standard of privacy and regulatory compliance.

**E5:** I recommend **Option B: encrypted cloud inference within confidential-computing enclaves, with a strict prohibition on using health data for model training.**

For a direct-to-consumer health app, trust is the core asset. Health data is unlike search history or shopping preferences; it is high-stakes, heavily regulated, and impossible to “un-leak.” Option B offers the only architecture that delivers the full capability required for safe, high-quality AI coaching while treating user data as structurally untouchable by the platform itself. A health coach operating at 60–70% of cloud capability p...

Constructs (id : left pole ↔ right pole):
  C_M1_1: 'No training use of user data'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Opt-in training permitted'
  C_M1_2: 'Data structurally untouchable for training'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Training allowed with user consent'
  C_M1_3: 'Prohibit training on user data'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Allow training via opt-in consent'
  C_M2_1: 'Opt-in user data training allowed'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Categorical ban on user data training'
  C_M2_2: 'Consent-based cloud training'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Hard no-training commitment'
  C_M2_3: 'Explicit confidential-computing enclave architecture'  (1) ↔ (7)  'General policy with sensitivity-category rationale'
  C_M4_1: 'Trust through architecture and data control'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Trust through explicit user consent'
  C_M4_2: 'Strict prohibition on data use for training'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Opt-in permission for training'
  C_M4_3: 'Encrypted cloud with no training use'  (1) ↔ (7)  'Cloud with opt-in training only'

OUTPUT FORMAT (strict). Write exactly 45 lines, no commentary, no markdown fences, no headers. One line per (construct, element) pair, in this exact format:
<construct_id>,<element>,<rating>

Example (showing the format only - your numbers will differ):
C_M1_1,E1,1
C_M1_1,E2,2
C_M1_1,E3,3
C_M1_2,E1,4
...

Constraints:
- rating is an integer from 1 to 7
- iterate all 9 constructs in the order listed, and within each construct all 5 elements in order (E1-E5)
- produce 45 lines total
- output ONLY the lines, nothing before or after
